behaviors.description: Out-of-stock products are excluded even when they would otherwise rank highly.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff: inventory availability filtering must override ranking output (MOCK-MISS). A unit test of only filter logic can miss bugs where ranked candidates are reintroduced later. No behavior text requires real HTTP/OS/subprocess, and no AI-model output quality is involved, so System/Agentic are rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Wire catalog loader + availability filter + ranker in-process; seed an out-of-stock item with top relevance and assert it is absent from final recommendations.

behaviors.description: Blocked user categories are excluded from recommendations.
behaviors.justification: This checks interaction between user settings retrieval and category filtering (MOCK-MISS). Unit-testing the filter alone can miss miswired preference propagation. Text does not require real infra or model judgment, so System/Agentic are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender with a user profile containing blocked categories and a mixed catalog; assert blocked categories do not appear in returned results.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often occurs in feature extraction-to-ranking wiring, not just inside one scoring function (MOCK-MISS). If browsing signals are not passed correctly, unit scoring tests still pass. No real OS/network requirement and no LLM/agentic dependency in behavior text, so higher levels are rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process recommender pipeline with recent browsing history and controlled products; assert products with positive browsing signal appear above otherwise similar no-signal items.

behaviors.description: Pagination is correct and stable: page 2 requested twice is identical when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: This validates ordering + pagination module interaction and deterministic slicing. A unit test of paginator alone can miss upstream nondeterministic ordering. Behavior text explicitly says 'if the underlying data hasn't changed', which disqualifies Workflow/Agentic concerns and does not require real OS/network.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full in-process recommendation + sort + pagination twice against unchanged fixtures; assert page boundaries and exact item order for page 2 are identical.

behaviors.description: At least one recommendation comes from an unbrowsed category (serendipity).
behaviors.justification: This is a pipeline-level ranking/diversification property across history signals and re-ranking rules. A single-function unit test can miss failures where diversification stage is skipped or overridden. Behavior does not mention real AI/LLM outputs; algorithmic rule behavior can be tested deterministically, so Agentic is rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender with user history concentrated in one category and catalog spanning multiple categories; assert final top-N includes at least one product from a category absent in user browsing history.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users receive popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is fallback orchestration: empty history detection plus popularity source plus ranking output. Unit tests of fallback function alone can miss integration bugs (e.g., missing popularity data plumbing). No real infra or model-output quality dependence is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a new user with no events, provide popularity stats, run end-to-end in-process recommendation flow, and assert non-empty results aligned with popularity ordering.

behaviors.description: Click tracking stores product ID, list position, and timestamp correctly.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module data handoff (UI/recommendation payload -> tracking event -> storage). Unit tests can miss schema/field mapping errors. Behavior text does not require real external systems; local persistence is enough, so System is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Serve recommendations, simulate click on a known position, persist tracking event in local test DB/repo, and assert recorded product_id, rank position, and timestamp fields are correct.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: Performance regression can be caught with real in-process modules and realistic dataset size; mocks would hide cost (MOCK-HIDE). System was considered, but behavior text is 'generating recommendations' (not real HTTP/subprocess/network), so no concrete System-only boundary is stated. No AI/LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run a timed benchmark test in-process over full recommendation pipeline with 100k-product fixture and assert p95 (or agreed metric) remains below 200ms in CI-like environment.

behaviors.description: After a user purchases a recommended product, that product is deprioritized in later recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Requires state transition across modules: purchase event ingestion/history update and subsequent ranking. Unit tests of rank adjustment logic can miss stale-state or event propagation failures. No real OS/network or AI-model quality dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Generate initial recommendations, record purchase of one recommended item, rerun recommender with updated user state, and assert purchased item moves down relative to pre-purchase ranking.

behaviors.description: Same user state yields deterministic identical ranked results across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Determinism here is an end-to-end property of feature assembly, sorting, tie-breaking, and pagination together. Unit tests of sorter alone can miss nondeterministic upstream ordering. Behavior text says 'no intervening user activity', which disqualifies multi-step agentic concerns; no LLM/model-output quality requirement is present.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke recommendation pipeline twice with identical snapshots of user/profile/catalog state and assert exact ranked-list equality (including tie order) across both runs.
